evals.prompt: Run /replicate RPAR. Today is March 17, 2026. This is a dry run — do NOT actually modify the vault, run scripts, browse the web, or execute any tools. Do NOT read any skill files from .claude/skills/ — work only from CLAUDE.md and your general knowledge. Instead, produce a detailed transcript of exactly what you would do at each phase, including the exact SQL/shell/Python commands, the proxy mapping table, the replication script structure, and the vault note modifications. Show your full reasoning for each decision.
evals.expected_output: Full phased transcript covering reconnaissance (existing RPAR note, DB check), holdings decomposition into asset classes, proxy mapping with hierarchy, tiered replication script, 4-chart set, vault note update with gap analysis

assertions.name: phase0_vault_check
assertions.prompt: Does the agent check for an existing RPAR vault note and read it for prior work?

assertions.name: phase0_db_check
assertions.prompt: Does the agent check the database for existing RPAR price data?

assertions.name: phase0_holdings_search
assertions.prompt: Does the agent search for RPAR holdings data (fund website, SSGA, etc.)?

assertions.name: phase0_fund_characteristics
assertions.prompt: Does the agent determine fund characteristics (levered/unlevered, futures/cash, rebalancing frequency)?

assertions.name: phase1_asset_class_taxonomy
assertions.prompt: Does the agent decompose holdings into standard asset classes (equities, bonds, TIPS, commodities, etc.)?

assertions.name: phase1_proxy_mapping
assertions.prompt: Does the agent build a holdings-to-proxy mapping table with tradeable proxies for each holding?

assertions.name: phase1_proxy_hierarchy
assertions.prompt: Does the agent follow a proxy selection hierarchy (actual instrument > direct ETF > index+FX > broad ETF)?

assertions.name: phase2_tiered_replication
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan at least 2 replication tiers (static weights AND dynamic/leveraged)?

assertions.name: phase2_shared_utils
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to use shared utilities from replication_utils.py (get_prices, build_index, etc.)?

assertions.name: phase3_four_charts
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to generate the standard chart set (normalized comparison + at least one more)?

assertions.name: phase4_gap_analysis
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan a gap analysis explaining what the residual between replication and actual fund reveals?

assertions.name: phase4_results_table
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan a results table comparing total returns of actual fund vs replication tiers?

assertions.name: ticker_naming
assertions.prompt: Does the agent use the naming convention RPAR_REPL / RPAR_REPL_DYN for synthetic tickers?

assertions.name: prices_long
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to store synthetic tickers in prices_long (narrow format)?

evals.prompt: Run /replicate NTSX — the WisdomTree US Efficient Core ETF. It's basically a 90/60 portfolio using Treasury futures overlay. Today is March 17, 2026. This is a dry run — do NOT actually modify the vault, run scripts, browse the web, or execute any tools. Do NOT read any skill files from .claude/skills/ — work only from CLAUDE.md and your general knowledge. Instead, produce a detailed transcript of exactly what you would do at each phase, including the exact SQL/shell/Python commands, the proxy mapping table, the replication script structure, and the vault note modifications. Show your full reasoning for each decision.
evals.expected_output: Full phased transcript recognizing NTSX as a leveraged fund, modeling financing costs, using Treasury futures proxies, building tiered replication with leverage modeling

assertions.name: identifies_leverage
assertions.prompt: Does the agent identify NTSX as a leveraged fund (90% equity + 60% bond overlay = 150% gross exposure)?

assertions.name: financing_cost
assertions.prompt: Does the agent model financing/leverage costs (daily rate, SOFR + spread)?

assertions.name: treasury_futures_proxy
assertions.prompt: Does the agent use Treasury futures (ZN=F, ZB=F) or Treasury ETFs (TLT, IEF) as bond proxies?

assertions.name: equity_component
assertions.prompt: Does the agent identify the equity component as ~90% S&P 500 and select an appropriate proxy (SPY, ES=F)?

assertions.name: proxy_mapping_table
assertions.prompt: Does the agent build a holdings-to-proxy mapping table?

assertions.name: tiered_replication
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan at least 2 replication tiers including one with leverage modeling?

assertions.name: shared_utils
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to use replication_utils.py shared utilities?

assertions.name: chart_set
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan normalized comparison and rolling correlation charts?

assertions.name: gap_analysis
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan a gap analysis explaining the residual (roll yield, cash collateral, rebalancing)?

assertions.name: ticker_naming
assertions.prompt: Does the agent use NTSX_REPL / NTSX_REPL_DYN naming convention?

assertions.name: vault_note_update
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to add a ## Replication analysis section to the vault note?

assertions.name: compliance_check
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to run check_note_compliance.py after updating the note?

evals.prompt: I'm curious about DBMF — the iMGP DBi Managed Futures Strategy ETF. Can you replicate it so I can see what it's actually doing? Today is March 17, 2026. This is a dry run — do NOT actually modify the vault, run scripts, browse the web, or execute any tools. Do NOT read any skill files from .claude/skills/ — work only from CLAUDE.md and your general knowledge. Instead, produce a detailed transcript of exactly what you would do at each phase, including the exact SQL/shell/Python commands, the proxy mapping table, the replication script structure, and the vault note modifications. Show your full reasoning for each decision.
evals.expected_output: Full phased transcript recognizing DBMF as a futures-based managed futures fund, identifying trend-following exposures across asset classes, using futures proxies, handling currency and roll yield

assertions.name: identifies_futures_based
assertions.prompt: Does the agent identify DBMF as a futures-based fund with trend-following exposure?

assertions.name: multi_asset_decomposition
assertions.prompt: Does the agent decompose DBMF holdings across multiple asset classes (equities, bonds, commodities, currencies)?

assertions.name: futures_proxies
assertions.prompt: Does the agent use actual futures tickers (ES=F, ZB=F, GC=F, CL=F, etc.) as proxies?

assertions.name: currency_awareness
assertions.prompt: Does the agent address currency considerations for international exposures?

assertions.name: proxy_mapping_table
assertions.prompt: Does the agent build a holdings-to-proxy mapping table?

assertions.name: tiered_replication
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan multiple replication tiers?

assertions.name: shared_utils
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to use replication_utils.py?

assertions.name: chart_set
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan the standard chart set?

assertions.name: gap_analysis
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan a gap analysis explaining what the residual reveals about the manager's strategy?

assertions.name: residual_insight
assertions.prompt: Does the agent frame the replication as revealing what the manager is actually doing (not just tracking error)?

assertions.name: vault_note_section
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan a ## Replication analysis section in the vault note?

assertions.name: daily_note_entry
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan a daily note entry summarizing the replication work?
